## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Surfaced by ₢BBAAc: depot enumeration via Cloud Billing v1 + Mason-SA gate is
  blind to DELETE_REQUESTED projects. Billing unlinked at unmake time before
  soft-delete; Mason SA list returns 404 on pending-delete. Operator has no
  visibility into ghost depots; canonical-establish moniker selector collides
  with reserved project IDs on retry-after-failure.
  
  ## Wrap criterion
  
  `rbw-dl` shows DELETE_REQUESTED alongside ACTIVE. `rbtdrk_pick_next_moniker`
  refreshes fact files load-bearingly before scan, no reliance on operator-run
  `rbw-dl`. RBGC kindle owns the displayName anchor
  (`RBGC_DEPOT_DISPLAY_PREFIX="RBGC_DEPOT_COMMON"`) across depot/Mason/Governor
  create sites + search site. Mason-SA discrimination removed; moniker derived by
  stripping prefix from displayName. CRM v3 `projects:search` replaces Cloud
  Billing enumeration. Family stem bumped past current pending-delete
  reservations. Specs RBSDE/RBSGM in sync with code.
  
  ## Constraints
  
  - Burn-the-bridges: all current depots deleted before this lands; no
    transitional dual-search code.
  - DisplayName-based discrimination; projectId-infix and label schemes out of
    scope.
  
  ## Cross-pace dependencies
  
  - Surfaced by ₢BBAAc; operator burn-the-bridges cleanup precedes landing.
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - Wider billing-list enumeration audit (other sites may share assumption).
  - ProjectId-infix migration scheme.
  - BBAAc walkthrough resumption (separate concern, blocked on this).

Register four calibrant fixtures driving the bash blackbox testbench: calibrant-verdicts (Independent, 4 cases — pass/fail/skip/pass_with_output writing output.txt distinct from engine's auto-written trace.txt), calibrant-fail-fast (Independent, 2 sections / 4 cases — §A [pass, fail, not_reached_intra] + §B [not_reached_inter] verifying intra-section and inter-section fail-fast under default mode), calibrant-progressing (StateProgressing, 2 cases — probe-Ok→Pass and hardcoded probe-Err with no env-var/file coupling), calibrant-sentinel (Independent, 1 case — writes ran.sentinel for suite-fail-fast pivot). Wire fixture-name consts in rbtdrm_manifest.rs (all four with Some(&[]) required-colophons since calibrant cases never shell out), disposition entries in rbtdrc_fixture_disposition (calibrant-progressing → StateProgressing, others → Independent default), sections lookup in rbtdrc_sections_for_fixture, lib.rs module + test-module declarations. Removes the dangling 'pub mod rbtdrd_dummy;' lib.rs declaration (the rbtdrd_dummy.rs source file was already deleted upstream). Helper rbtdrl_write_sentinel centralizes the 3-call sentinel write and fails visibly on I/O error — silent failure would let the blackbox driver conclude the case wasn't reached when actually it was. 17 unit tests in rbtdtl_calibrant.rs pin manifest entries (4 empty), dispositions (3 Independent + 1 StateProgressing), sections registration (case counts and section names), per-case verdicts, sentinel write/non-write contracts, probe-Err diagnostic format ('precondition' + 'remediation:'). Tests look up cases via rbtdrc_sections_for_fixture rather than calling fns directly — exercising the same dispatch path the engine uses, so a registration-without-implementation regression is caught. RCG-compliant: no pace/coronet references in comments, pub(crate) for cross-test consts (RBTDRL_SENTINEL_FILE, RBTDRL_OUTPUT_FILE) matching rbtdrk_canonical precedent, pub static for sections, internal case fns kept private. Locked decisions held: output.txt vs engine trace.txt distinction, no per-fixture tabtargets, no RBTDRM_SUITE_CALIBRANT const (suite-level fail-fast comes from existing rbte_engine.sh set -euo pipefail), hardcoded probe-Err deterministic. Build clean; 86/86 unit tests pass (69 prior + 17 new); fast TestSuite 72 fast-fixture passes + baseline rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels carry-forward (same as BBAAe). End-to-end binary dispatch verified: calibrant-verdicts fail-fasts after first fail, calibrant-sentinel writes both ran.sentinel and engine-auto trace.txt, calibrant-progressing exercises both probe paths.

Wire fixture-name consts in rbtdrm_manifest.rs (all four with Some(&[]) required-colophons since calibrant cases never shell out), disposition entries in rbtdrc_fixture_disposition (calibrant-progressing → StateProgressing, others → Independent default), sections lookup in rbtdrc_sections_for_fixture, lib.rs module + test-module declarations. Removes the dangling 'pub mod rbtdrd_dummy;' lib.rs declaration (the rbtdrd_dummy.rs source file was already deleted upstream). Helper rbtdrl_write_sentinel centralizes the 3-call sentinel write and fails visibly on I/O error — silent failure would let the blackbox driver conclude the case wasn't reached when actually it was. 17 unit tests in rbtdtl_calibrant.rs pin manifest entries (4 empty), dispositions (3 Independent + 1 StateProgressing), sections registration (case counts and section names), per-case verdicts, sentinel write/non-write contracts, probe-Err diagnostic format ('precondition' + 'remediation:'). Tests look up cases via rbtdrc_sections_for_fixture rather than calling fns directly — exercising the same dispatch path the engine uses, so a registration-without-implementation regression is caught. RCG-compliant: no pace/coronet references in comments, pub(crate) for cross-test consts (RBTDRL_SENTINEL_FILE, RBTDRL_OUTPUT_FILE) matching rbtdrk_canonical precedent, pub static for sections, internal case fns kept private. Locked decisions held: output.txt vs engine trace.txt distinction, no per-fixture tabtargets, no RBTDRM_SUITE_CALIBRANT const (suite-level fail-fast comes from existing rbte_engine.sh set -euo pipefail), hardcoded probe-Err deterministic. Build clean; 86/86 unit tests pass (69 prior + 17 new); fast TestSuite 72 fast-fixture passes + baseline rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels carry-forward (same as BBAAe). End-to-end binary dispatch verified: calibrant-verdicts fail-fasts after first fail, calibrant-sentinel writes both ran.sentinel and engine-auto trace.txt, calibrant-progressing exercises both probe paths.

1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BB
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.
6	
7	This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.
8	
9	## Single operator prerequisite chain
10	
11	Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.
12	
13	## Entry contract — load-bearing
14	
15	`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:
16	
17	- Working tree clean
18	- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
19	- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
20	- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
21	- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
22	- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files
23	
24	Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.
25	
26	## Failure mode contract
27	
28	Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.
29	
30	## Release-branch execution contract
31	
32	Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.
33	
34	**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.
35	
36	**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.
37	
38	**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.
39	
40	Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.
41	
42	## Tier layering
43	
44	Three tiers, escalating cost:
45	
46	| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
47	|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
48	| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
49	| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
50	| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |
51	
52	`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.
53	
54	## Gauntlet test suite — design
55	
56	`rbw-tP` exec's the **gauntlet suite** — a TestSuite (not a custom orchestrator script) composed of fixtures sequenced from marshal-zero state through canonical-credentialed state to crucible verification. The TestSuite mechanism is existing infrastructure (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.{fast,service,crucible,complete}.sh`); the gauntlet suite extends it.
57	
58	**Suite-of-fixtures, not orchestrator-script.** The original construction plan (custom bash orchestrator with inline tabtarget calls) was abandoned in favor of suite composition. Each section of the release-qualification sequence becomes a fixture; the suite composes them in order.
59	
60	**Naming hygiene.** "Gauntlet" is the suite name; "pristine" is reserved for the §1 pristine-lifecycle fixture and informally for tier vocabulary. The suite name does not appear at fixture or case level — distinct stratification across naming layers (suite ≠ fixture ≠ case).
61	
62	**State ladder, fixtures along it.** The suite walks a state ladder no single fixture covers:
63	
64	1. enrollment-validation (preflight, state-indifferent)
65	2. pristine-lifecycle (§1: marshal-zero gate + throwaway depot/SA lifecycle)
66	3. canonical-establish (§2: canonical depot levy + governor mantle + retriever invest + director invest + IAM propagation wait)
67	4. canonical-onboarding-sequence (§3: reliquary inscribe + per-vessel hallmark ordain — mimics operator onboarding journey under theurge control)
68	5. regime-validation, regime-smoke (post-§3, regimes now populated)
69	6. four-mode (cloud-build mode coverage)
70	7. tadmor, moriah, srjcl, pluml (§4: crucible suite)
71	
72	No automatic teardown — see "No-teardown decision" below.
73	
74	## Fixture disposition — Independent vs StateProgressing
75	
76	Each fixture declares a disposition flag in Rust, controlling engine behavior:
77	
78	- **Independent** — cases are self-contained; suite-order is informational. Engine permits keep-going mode for surveying.
79	- **StateProgressing** — case N's success establishes preconditions for case N+1. Engine refuses keep-going mode (incoherent: failed case leaves broken precondition). Fail-fast required.
80	
81	**Default is Independent.** Existing fixtures (`enrollment-validation`, `regime-validation`, `regime-smoke`, `four-mode`, `tadmor`, `moriah`, `srjcl`, `pluml`) are Independent and require no tagging change. Only fixtures whose case-N success establishes case-N+1 preconditions get StateProgressing — currently `pristine-lifecycle`, `canonical-establish`, and `canonical-onboarding-sequence`.
82	
83	Suite-level disposition derives from membership: any StateProgressing fixture in the suite makes the suite state-progressing → suite-level keep-going refused.
84	
85	**Per-case precondition probes.** Each case in a StateProgressing fixture probes its precondition at start. If state matches expectation, run. If not, refuse with operator-actionable diagnostic naming the expected state and the closest fixture that produces it. This enables safe a-la-carte single-case rerun — the engine doesn't need to know the case's history; the case's probe enforces.
86	
87	## Suite-level fail-fast
88	
89	Across all suites — not just gauntlet — any fixture failure stops subsequent fixtures from running automatically. State-progressing suites require this for correctness; independent suites adopt it for the simpler mental model. Operators who want full surveying run individual fixtures separately.
90	
91	## No-teardown decision
92	
93	The gauntlet suite omits an automatic teardown phase. After green tally, the canonical depot, SAs, hallmarks, and RBRA files persist for operator inspection. Deliberate trade-off:
94	
95	- **Inspect-after-success** is genuine operator value — browse canonical depot in GCP console, inspect GAR contents, run ad-hoc verification a test sequence cannot anticipate.
96	- **Cleanup is operator-driven** — manual sequence is `rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU` (governor cleanup folds into unmake per BBAAN). All existing tabtargets; no new infrastructure.
97	- **Failed runs don't accumulate cleanup debt** — under fail-fast, teardown wouldn't run anyway; the no-teardown decision only changes behavior on full success.
98	- **Cost visibility** — pending-delete soft-deleted projects accumulate per run (RELEASE.md accepts this); not changed by teardown decision.
99	
100	Documentation of the post-success cleanup ceremony is BBAAI work (RELEASE.md).
101	
102	## Operator scope
103	
104	Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section.
105	
106	## Coupling to ₣A_
107	
108	Cutover work from ₣A_ informing `rbw-tP`'s sequence has landed (BBAAM depot-identity-collapse). ₣A_'s remaining paces run independently of BB.
109	
110	## Locked design decisions — pointers
111	
112	- **Depot identity collapse** (BBAAM, landed): RBRR collapses to `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`; `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows through depot-affiliated resources; per-moniker depot fact-files; pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust (`rbtdrp_pristine.rs`), not payor.
113	- **Cult-verb naming** (BBAAN/BBAAQ/BBAAR, landed): SA domain muster→**roster**; image domain muster→**audit**. Domain-exclusive split; lowercase tail letters preserved for no-cloud-change observation colophons.
114	
115	## Calibrant fixture family — BBAAh
116	
117	Synthetic Rust fixtures with deterministic verdicts, registered in the manifest by BBAAh. The family exists to give ₣BC `rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test` deterministic targets for bash-blackbox driving and manifest-coverage validation; here in ₣BB only the foundation lands.
118	
119	**Anchor word: `calibrant`.** Module `rbtdrl_calibrant.rs` (classifier `l` for caLibrant since `c` is owned by crucible). Replaces the dormant `rbtdrd_dummy.rs` placeholder. Test counterpart `rbtdtl_calibrant.rs`. Internal-only vocabulary; not end-user-facing.
120	
121	**Fixture catalog** (Rust, manifest-registered):
122	
123	- `calibrant-verdicts` (Independent) — pass / fail / skip / pass_with_output. Verdict-path coverage. The case-written file (`output.txt`) is distinct from the engine-auto `trace.txt`.
124	- `calibrant-fail-fast` (Independent, multi-section) — fixture-internal fail-fast across cases and across sections; "not_reached" cases write sentinel files used as absence-of-side-effect assertions.
125	- `calibrant-progressing` (StateProgressing) — exercises `rbtdrb_Probe` diagnostic format and `rbtdre_resolve_fail_fast` policy gate.
126	- `calibrant-sentinel` (Independent) — single case writing a known file when it runs. Used for suite-level fail-fast verification.
127	
128	Suite: `calibrant` = [verdicts, fail-fast, sentinel]. Sentinel-after-fail verifies cross-fixture fail-fast contract.
129	
130	Downstream consumption — bash testbench, manifest-coverage runtime checks, coverage-validation fixtures and driver — lives in ₣BC.
131	
132	## References
133	
134	- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
135	- ₣BC rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test — calibrant downstream consumers
136	- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
137	- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
138	- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)
139	
140	# jjezs_pace ₢BBAAm
141	
142	## Character
143	
144	Surfaced by ₢BBAAc: depot enumeration via Cloud Billing v1 + Mason-SA gate is
145	blind to DELETE_REQUESTED projects. Billing unlinked at unmake time before
146	soft-delete; Mason SA list returns 404 on pending-delete. Operator has no
147	visibility into ghost depots; canonical-establish moniker selector collides
148	with reserved project IDs on retry-after-failure.
149	
150	## Wrap criterion
151	
152	`rbw-dl` shows DELETE_REQUESTED alongside ACTIVE. `rbtdrk_pick_next_moniker`
153	refreshes fact files load-bearingly before scan, no reliance on operator-run
154	`rbw-dl`. RBGC kindle owns the displayName anchor
155	(`RBGC_DEPOT_DISPLAY_PREFIX="RBGC_DEPOT_COMMON"`) across depot/Mason/Governor
156	create sites + search site. Mason-SA discrimination removed; moniker derived by
157	stripping prefix from displayName. CRM v3 `projects:search` replaces Cloud
158	Billing enumeration. Family stem bumped past current pending-delete
159	reservations. Specs RBSDE/RBSGM in sync with code.
160	
161	## Constraints
162	
163	- Burn-the-bridges: all current depots deleted before this lands; no
164	  transitional dual-search code.
165	- DisplayName-based discrimination; projectId-infix and label schemes out of
166	  scope.
167	
168	## Cross-pace dependencies
169	
170	- Surfaced by ₢BBAAc; operator burn-the-bridges cleanup precedes landing.
171	
172	## Out of scope
173	
174	- Wider billing-list enumeration audit (other sites may share assumption).
175	- ProjectId-infix migration scheme.
176	- BBAAc walkthrough resumption (separate concern, blocked on this).

| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |

`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.

## Gauntlet test suite — design

`rbw-tP` exec's the **gauntlet suite** — a TestSuite (not a custom orchestrator script) composed of fixtures sequenced from marshal-zero state through canonical-credentialed state to crucible verification. The TestSuite mechanism is existing infrastructure (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.{fast,service,crucible,complete}.sh`); the gauntlet suite extends it.

**Suite-of-fixtures, not orchestrator-script.** The original construction plan (custom bash orchestrator with inline tabtarget calls) was abandoned in favor of suite composition. Each section of the release-qualification sequence becomes a fixture; the suite composes them in order.

**Naming hygiene.** "Gauntlet" is the suite name; "pristine" is reserved for the §1 pristine-lifecycle fixture and informally for tier vocabulary. The suite name does not appear at fixture or case level — distinct stratification across naming layers (suite ≠ fixture ≠ case).

**State ladder, fixtures along it.** The suite walks a state ladder no single fixture covers:

1. enrollment-validation (preflight, state-indifferent)
2. pristine-lifecycle (§1: marshal-zero gate + throwaway depot/SA lifecycle)
3. canonical-establish (§2: canonical depot levy + governor mantle + retriever invest + director invest + IAM propagation wait)
4. canonical-onboarding-sequence (§3: reliquary inscribe + per-vessel hallmark ordain — mimics operator onboarding journey under theurge control)
5. regime-validation, regime-smoke (post-§3, regimes now populated)
6. four-mode (cloud-build mode coverage)
7. tadmor, moriah, srjcl, pluml (§4: crucible suite)

No automatic teardown — see "No-teardown decision" below.

## Fixture disposition — Independent vs StateProgressing

Each fixture declares a disposition flag in Rust, controlling engine behavior:

- **Independent** — cases are self-contained; suite-order is informational. Engine permits keep-going mode for surveying.
- **StateProgressing** — case N's success establishes preconditions for case N+1. Engine refuses keep-going mode (incoherent: failed case leaves broken precondition). Fail-fast required.

**Default is Independent.** Existing fixtures (`enrollment-validation`, `regime-validation`, `regime-smoke`, `four-mode`, `tadmor`, `moriah`, `srjcl`, `pluml`) are Independent and require no tagging change. Only fixtures whose case-N success establishes case-N+1 preconditions get StateProgressing — currently `pristine-lifecycle`, `canonical-establish`, and `canonical-onboarding-sequence`.

Suite-level disposition derives from membership: any StateProgressing fixture in the suite makes the suite state-progressing → suite-level keep-going refused.

**Per-case precondition probes.** Each case in a StateProgressing fixture probes its precondition at start. If state matches expectation, run. If not, refuse with operator-actionable diagnostic naming the expected state and the closest fixture that produces it. This enables safe a-la-carte single-case rerun — the engine doesn't need to know the case's history; the case's probe enforces.

## Suite-level fail-fast

Across all suites — not just gauntlet — any fixture failure stops subsequent fixtures from running automatically. State-progressing suites require this for correctness; independent suites adopt it for the simpler mental model. Operators who want full surveying run individual fixtures separately.

## No-teardown decision

The gauntlet suite omits an automatic teardown phase. After green tally, the canonical depot, SAs, hallmarks, and RBRA files persist for operator inspection. Deliberate trade-off:

- **Inspect-after-success** is genuine operator value — browse canonical depot in GCP console, inspect GAR contents, run ad-hoc verification a test sequence cannot anticipate.
- **Cleanup is operator-driven** — manual sequence is `rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU` (governor cleanup folds into unmake per BBAAN). All existing tabtargets; no new infrastructure.
- **Failed runs don't accumulate cleanup debt** — under fail-fast, teardown wouldn't run anyway; the no-teardown decision only changes behavior on full success.
- **Cost visibility** — pending-delete soft-deleted projects accumulate per run (RELEASE.md accepts this); not changed by teardown decision.

Documentation of the post-success cleanup ceremony is BBAAI work (RELEASE.md).

## Operator scope

Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section.

## Coupling to ₣A_

Cutover work from ₣A_ informing `rbw-tP`'s sequence has landed (BBAAM depot-identity-collapse). ₣A_'s remaining paces run independently of BB.

## Locked design decisions — pointers

- **Depot identity collapse** (BBAAM, landed): RBRR collapses to `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`; `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows through depot-affiliated resources; per-moniker depot fact-files; pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust (`rbtdrp_pristine.rs`), not payor.
- **Cult-verb naming** (BBAAN/BBAAQ/BBAAR, landed): SA domain muster→**roster**; image domain muster→**audit**. Domain-exclusive split; lowercase tail letters preserved for no-cloud-change observation colophons.

## Calibrant fixture family — BBAAh

Synthetic Rust fixtures with deterministic verdicts, registered in the manifest by BBAAh. The family exists to give ₣BC `rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test` deterministic targets for bash-blackbox driving and manifest-coverage validation; here in ₣BB only the foundation lands.

**Anchor word: `calibrant`.** Module `rbtdrl_calibrant.rs` (classifier `l` for caLibrant since `c` is owned by crucible). Replaces the dormant `rbtdrd_dummy.rs` placeholder. Test counterpart `rbtdtl_calibrant.rs`. Internal-only vocabulary; not end-user-facing.

**Fixture catalog** (Rust, manifest-registered):

- `calibrant-verdicts` (Independent) — pass / fail / skip / pass_with_output. Verdict-path coverage. The case-written file (`output.txt`) is distinct from the engine-auto `trace.txt`.
- `calibrant-fail-fast` (Independent, multi-section) — fixture-internal fail-fast across cases and across sections; "not_reached" cases write sentinel files used as absence-of-side-effect assertions.
- `calibrant-progressing` (StateProgressing) — exercises `rbtdrb_Probe` diagnostic format and `rbtdre_resolve_fail_fast` policy gate.
- `calibrant-sentinel` (Independent) — single case writing a known file when it runs. Used for suite-level fail-fast verification.

Suite: `calibrant` = [verdicts, fail-fast, sentinel]. Sentinel-after-fail verifies cross-fixture fail-fast contract.

Downstream consumption — bash testbench, manifest-coverage runtime checks, coverage-validation fixtures and driver — lives in ₣BC.

## References

- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
- ₣BC rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test — calibrant downstream consumers
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

# jjezs_pace ₢BBAAm

## Character

Surfaced by ₢BBAAc: depot enumeration via Cloud Billing v1 + Mason-SA gate is
blind to DELETE_REQUESTED projects. Billing unlinked at unmake time before
soft-delete; Mason SA list returns 404 on pending-delete. Operator has no
visibility into ghost depots; canonical-establish moniker selector collides
with reserved project IDs on retry-after-failure.

## Wrap criterion

`rbw-dl` shows DELETE_REQUESTED alongside ACTIVE. `rbtdrk_pick_next_moniker`
refreshes fact files load-bearingly before scan, no reliance on operator-run
`rbw-dl`. RBGC kindle owns the displayName anchor
(`RBGC_DEPOT_DISPLAY_PREFIX="RBGC_DEPOT_COMMON"`) across depot/Mason/Governor
create sites + search site. Mason-SA discrimination removed; moniker derived by
stripping prefix from displayName. CRM v3 `projects:search` replaces Cloud
Billing enumeration. Family stem bumped past current pending-delete
reservations. Specs RBSDE/RBSGM in sync with code.

## Constraints

- Burn-the-bridges: all current depots deleted before this lands; no
  transitional dual-search code.
- DisplayName-based discrimination; projectId-infix and label schemes out of
  scope.

## Cross-pace dependencies

- Surfaced by ₢BBAAc; operator burn-the-bridges cleanup precedes landing.

## Out of scope

- Wider billing-list enumeration audit (other sites may share assumption).
- ProjectId-infix migration scheme.
- BBAAc walkthrough resumption (separate concern, blocked on this).

content.text:
Mounted ₢BBAAm. Here's the context.

## Heat ₣BB — rbk-mvp-3-release-qualification (22/29 done)

**Paddock summary**: This heat builds `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third release tier that refuses to run unless marshal-zero state was just committed. Constructs a gauntlet test suite walking from marshal-zero → canonical-credentialed state → crucible verification. Recent work landed canonical-establish fixture and BUK param1-channel fix (BBAAc).

**Recent commits**:
- `5baf368f` ₢BBAAc — Fix BUK param1-channel violation in 4 Governor invest/divest fns
- `776515a5` — canonical-establish fixture: set `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER=canest100000`
- `b9f948db` — canonical-establish fixture: install canonical RBRR prefixes (canc-/canr-)
- `80dbf492` — Marshal Zero release qualification reset

## Pace ₢BBAAm — depot-list-via-displayname-search (rough)

**Surfaced by**: ₢BBAAc — depot enumeration via Cloud Billing v1 + Mason-SA gate is blind to DELETE_REQUESTED projects. Operator has no visibility into ghost depots; canonical-establish moniker selector collides with reserved project IDs on retry-after-failure.

**Wrap criterion (six load-bearing pieces)**:
1. `rbw-dl` shows DELETE_REQUESTED alongside ACTIVE
2. `rbtdrk_pick_next_moniker` refreshes fact files load-bearingly before scan
3. RBGC kindle owns `RBGC_DEPOT_DISPLAY_PREFIX="RBGC_DEPOT_COMMON"` anchor across depot/Mason/Governor create sites + search site
4. Mason-SA discrimination removed; moniker derived by stripping prefix from displayName
5. CRM v3 `projects:search` replaces Cloud Billing v1 enumeration
6. Family stem bumped past current pending-delete reservations; RBSDE/RBSGM specs synced

**Constraints**: Burn-the-bridges (all current depots deleted before landing; no transitional dual-search). DisplayName-based discrimination only.

## Name assessment

Silks "depot-list-via-displayname-search" captures the pivot but understates scope — the docket is broader (also includes Mason-SA removal, fact-file refresh discipline, family stem bump, spec sync). Acceptable as a shorthand for the central enabling change. **Keep.**

## Proposed approach

1. **RBGC kindle** — add `RBGC_DEPOT_DISPLAY_PREFIX` constant; thread to depot/Mason/Governor create sites
2. **Swap enumeration backend** — replace Cloud Billing v1 list with CRM v3 `projects:search` filtered by displayName, surfacing both ACTIVE and DELETE_REQUESTED states
3. **Strip Mason-SA discrimination** — derive moniker by stripping the displayName prefix; delete the SA-list gate
4. **Family stem bump + fact-file refresh** — adjust `rbtdrk_pick_next_moniker` to re-fetch fact files before scanning; bump stem past current pending-delete reservations
5. **Spec sync** — RBSDE (depot_levy) and RBSGM (governor_mantle) reflect new flow

**Execution strategy**: Sequential opus — coupled changes across rbgc/rbgp/rbgb/rbgd/rbtdrk plus two specs. Many call sites touched but each change is design-judgment-bearing, not mechanical. Burn-the-bridges constraint means I should verify no current depots exist before landing (operator-confirmed precondition).

Ready to proceed? Or do you want to adjust the approach first?

message.content: proceed.  Try to use sonnet agents in parallel to do the work, and check it after, for speed.

130	  # Load RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID from environment
131	  test -n "${RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID:-}" || buc_die "RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID not set in environment"
132	  
133	  # Exchange refresh token for access token
134	  local z_access_token
135	  z_access_token=$(zrbgp_refresh_capture) || buc_die "Failed to exchange OAuth refresh token"
136	  
137	  test -n "${z_access_token}" || buc_die "Empty access token from OAuth exchange"
138	  
139	  buc_log_args "Payor OAuth authentication successful"
140	  echo "${z_access_token}"
141	}
142	
143	# Probe per-installation depot states and emit per-moniker fact files.
144	# Classification is by Mason SA presence — any billing-account project hosting
145	# a service account matching ^mason-[a-z][a-z0-9]*@ is a depot.  The moniker
146	# is derived from the Mason SA local-part after stripping the mason- prefix.
147	# Lifecycle is determined first via CRM v1; only ACTIVE and DELETE_REQUESTED
148	# projects are candidates for SA-list scanning.  Per-moniker fact files
149	# named "<moniker>.${RBCC_fact_ext_depot}" (state) and
150	# "<moniker>.${RBCC_fact_ext_depot_project}" (project_id) are written via
151	# buf_write_fact_multi; consumers walk the emitted files in BURD_OUTPUT_DIR /
152	# BURD_TEMP_DIR (filesystem is the data bus).
153	#
154	# Mason SA presence IS the discriminator; projects without a Mason SA are
155	# silently skipped (no BROKEN state emitted).
156	#
157	# Billing v1 projects list and IAM v1 serviceAccounts list are paginated via
158	# nextPageToken; per-page infixes keep response files distinct.
159	#
160	# Single-shot per dispatch — buf_write_fact_multi dies on preexist.
161	#
162	# Args: <oauth_access_token>
163	zrbgp_depot_state_emit() {
164	  zrbgp_sentinel
165	
166	  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
167	  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "zrbgp_depot_state_emit: token required"
168	
169	  # RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID is min=0 in the regime (optional for payor-only
170	  # operations) but required here to enumerate depot projects.
171	  test -n "${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID:-}" \
172	    || buc_die "RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID is not set — required for depot_list"
173	
174	  local -r z_billing_url_base="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBILLING}${RBGC_CLOUDBILLING_V1}/billingAccounts/${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID}/projects"
175	
176	  # Per-iteration synthesized locals (BCG exception 2 — declare outside, assign inside)
177	  local z_billing_page=1
178	  local z_billing_page_token=""
179	  local z_billing_url=""
180	  local z_billing_tok_enc=""
181	  local z_billing_infix=""
182	  local z_project_count=0
183	  local z_index=0
184	  local z_global_project_index=0
185	  local z_project_id=""
186	  local z_crm_infix=""
187	  local z_crm_code=""
188	  local z_lifecycle_state=""
189	  local z_state=""
190	  local z_sa_url_base=""
191	  local z_sa_url=""
192	  local z_sa_page=1
193	  local z_sa_page_token=""
194	  local z_sa_tok_enc=""
195	  local z_sa_infix=""
196	  local z_sa_list_code=""
197	  local z_sa_count=0
198	  local z_sa_index=0
199	  local z_sa_email=""
200	  local z_mason_email=""
201	  local z_mason_local=""
202	  local z_moniker=""
203	
204	  while :; do
205	    z_billing_url="${z_billing_url_base}"
206	    if test -n "${z_billing_page_token}"; then
207	      z_billing_tok_enc=$(rbgu_urlencode_capture "${z_billing_page_token}") \
208	        || buc_die "Failed to URL-encode Billing v1 pageToken"
209	      z_billing_url="${z_billing_url}?pageToken=${z_billing_tok_enc}"
210	    fi
211	
212	    z_billing_infix="depot_state_billing_${z_billing_page}"
213	    rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_billing_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_billing_infix}"
214	    rbgu_http_require_ok "List billing account projects" "${z_billing_infix}"
215	
216	    z_project_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_billing_infix}" '.projectBillingInfo // [] | length') \
217	      || z_project_count=0
218	
219	    z_index=0
220	    while test "${z_index}" -lt "${z_project_count}"; do
221	      z_project_id=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_billing_infix}" ".projectBillingInfo[${z_index}].projectId") \
222	        || { z_index=$((z_index + 1)); z_global_project_index=$((z_global_project_index + 1)); continue; }
223	
224	      # CRM v1 lifecycle lookup gates the SA list — cheaper call, single
225	      # classification decision before potentially-failing IAM scan.
226	      z_crm_infix="depot_state_crm_${z_global_project_index}"
227	      rbgu_http_json "GET" \
228	        "${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/projects/${z_project_id}" \
229	        "${z_token}" "${z_crm_infix}" || true
230	
231	      z_crm_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_crm_infix}") || z_crm_code=""
232	      if test "${z_crm_code}" != "200"; then
233	        buc_log_args "Skipping project (CRM lookup HTTP ${z_crm_code}): ${z_project_id}"
234	        z_index=$((z_index + 1))
235	        z_global_project_index=$((z_global_project_index + 1))
236	        continue
237	      fi
238	
239	      z_lifecycle_state=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_crm_infix}" '.lifecycleState') \
240	        || z_lifecycle_state=""
241	
242	      if test "${z_lifecycle_state}" = "DELETE_REQUESTED"; then
243	        z_state="${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED}"
244	      elif test "${z_lifecycle_state}" = "ACTIVE"; then
245	        z_state="${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE}"
246	      else
247	        buc_log_args "Skipping project ${z_project_id} with unrecognized lifecycle state: ${z_lifecycle_state}"
248	        z_index=$((z_index + 1))
249	        z_global_project_index=$((z_global_project_index + 1))
250	        continue
251	      fi
252	
253	      # Scan SA list for a Mason SA — moniker portion anchored to RBRR's
254	      # ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$ shape.  Paginated via nextPageToken; break out on
255	      # first hit since the depot's Mason SA is a singleton.
256	      z_sa_url_base="${RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM}${RBGC_IAM_V1}/projects/${z_project_id}/serviceAccounts"
257	      z_sa_page=1
258	      z_sa_page_token=""
259	      z_mason_email=""
260	
261	      while :; do
262	        z_sa_url="${z_sa_url_base}"
263	        if test -n "${z_sa_page_token}"; then
264	          z_sa_tok_enc=$(rbgu_urlencode_capture "${z_sa_page_token}") \
265	            || buc_die "Failed to URL-encode IAM v1 pageToken"
266	          z_sa_url="${z_sa_url}?pageToken=${z_sa_tok_enc}"
267	        fi
268	
269	        z_sa_infix="depot_state_sa_${z_global_project_index}_${z_sa_page}"
270	        rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_sa_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_sa_infix}" || true
271	
272	        z_sa_list_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_sa_infix}") || z_sa_list_code=""
273	        if test "${z_sa_list_code}" != "200"; then
274	          buc_log_args "Skipping project (SA list HTTP ${z_sa_list_code}): ${z_project_id}"
275	          break
276	        fi
277	
278	        z_sa_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_sa_infix}" '.accounts // [] | length') \
279	          || z_sa_count=0
280	
281	        z_sa_index=0
282	        while test "${z_sa_index}" -lt "${z_sa_count}"; do
283	          z_sa_email=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_sa_infix}" ".accounts[${z_sa_index}].email") \
284	            || { z_sa_index=$((z_sa_index + 1)); continue; }
285	          if [[ "${z_sa_email}" =~ ^${RBCC_role_mason}-[a-z][a-z0-9]*@[^@]*\.iam\.gserviceaccount\.com$ ]]; then
286	            z_mason_email="${z_sa_email}"
287	            break
288	          fi
289	          z_sa_index=$((z_sa_index + 1))
290	        done
291	
292	        test -z "${z_mason_email}" || break
293	
294	        z_sa_page_token=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_sa_infix}" '.nextPageToken') \
295	          || z_sa_page_token=""
296	        test -n "${z_sa_page_token}" || break
297	        z_sa_page=$((z_sa_page + 1))
298	      done
299	
300	      if test -z "${z_mason_email}"; then
301	        buc_log_args "Skipping project — no Mason SA found: ${z_project_id}"
302	        z_index=$((z_index + 1))
303	        z_global_project_index=$((z_global_project_index + 1))
304	        continue
305	      fi
306	
307	      # Derive moniker from Mason SA local-part (strip leading mason- prefix)
308	      z_mason_local="${z_mason_email%%@*}"
309	      z_moniker="${z_mason_local#"${RBCC_role_mason}-"}"
310	
311	      buf_write_fact_multi "${z_moniker}" "${RBCC_fact_ext_depot}"         "${z_state}"
312	      buf_write_fact_multi "${z_moniker}" "${RBCC_fact_ext_depot_project}" "${z_project_id}"
313	
314	      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
315	      z_global_project_index=$((z_global_project_index + 1))
316	    done
317	
318	    z_billing_page_token=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_billing_infix}" '.nextPageToken') \
319	      || z_billing_page_token=""
320	    test -n "${z_billing_page_token}" || break
321	    z_billing_page=$((z_billing_page + 1))
322	  done
323	}
324	
325	# Post-lifecycle hook: refresh per-moniker depot fact files.
326	# Called by levy and unmake at end-of-operation so observers (theurge
327	# autodetect, manual inspection) see fresh state.
328	zrbgp_depot_list_update() {
329	  zrbgp_sentinel
330	
331	  buc_log_args "Refreshing per-moniker depot fact files"
332	
333	  local z_token
334	  z_token=$(zrbgp_authenticate_capture) || buc_die "Failed to authenticate for depot fact refresh"
335	
336	  zrbgp_depot_state_emit "${z_token}"
337	}
338	
339	######################################################################
340	# External Functions (rbgp_*)
341	
342	zrbgp_billing_attach() {
343	  zrbgp_sentinel
344	
345	  local -r z_billing_account="${1:-}"
346	
347	  buc_doc_brief "Attach a billing account to the project"
348	  buc_doc_param "billing_account" "Billing account ID to attach"
349	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
350	
351	  test -n "${z_billing_account}" || buc_die "Billing account ID required"
352	
353	  buc_step "Attaching billing account: ${z_billing_account}"
354	
355	  local z_token
356	  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"
357	  local -r z_billing_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_billing_attach.json"
358	  jq -n --arg billingAccountName "billingAccounts/${z_billing_account}" \
359	    --arg projectId "${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}" \
360	    '{
361	      billingAccountName: $billingAccountName,
362	      projectId: $projectId,
363	      billingEnabled: true
364	    }' > "${z_billing_body}" || buc_die "Failed to build billing attach body"
365	
366	  local -r z_billing_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}:setBillingInfo"
367	  rbgu_http_json "PUT" "${z_billing_url}" "${z_token}" \
368	                                  "${ZRBGP_INFIX_BILLING_ATTACH}" "${z_billing_body}"
369	  rbgu_http_require_ok "Attach billing account" "${ZRBGP_INFIX_BILLING_ATTACH}"
370	
371	  buc_success "Billing account ${z_billing_account} attached to project"
372	}
373	
374	zrbgp_billing_detach() {
375	  zrbgp_sentinel
376	
377	  buc_doc_brief "Detach billing account from the project"
378	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
379	
380	  buc_step "Detaching billing account from project"
381

file.content:
# Load RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID from environment
  test -n "${RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID:-}" || buc_die "RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID not set in environment"
  
  # Exchange refresh token for access token
  local z_access_token
  z_access_token=$(zrbgp_refresh_capture) || buc_die "Failed to exchange OAuth refresh token"
  
  test -n "${z_access_token}" || buc_die "Empty access token from OAuth exchange"
  
  buc_log_args "Payor OAuth authentication successful"
  echo "${z_access_token}"
}

# Probe per-installation depot states and emit per-moniker fact files.
# Classification is by Mason SA presence — any billing-account project hosting
# a service account matching ^mason-[a-z][a-z0-9]*@ is a depot.  The moniker
# is derived from the Mason SA local-part after stripping the mason- prefix.
# Lifecycle is determined first via CRM v1; only ACTIVE and DELETE_REQUESTED
# projects are candidates for SA-list scanning.  Per-moniker fact files
# named "<moniker>.${RBCC_fact_ext_depot}" (state) and
# "<moniker>.${RBCC_fact_ext_depot_project}" (project_id) are written via
# buf_write_fact_multi; consumers walk the emitted files in BURD_OUTPUT_DIR /
# BURD_TEMP_DIR (filesystem is the data bus).
#
# Mason SA presence IS the discriminator; projects without a Mason SA are
# silently skipped (no BROKEN state emitted).
#
# Billing v1 projects list and IAM v1 serviceAccounts list are paginated via
# nextPageToken; per-page infixes keep response files distinct.
#
# Single-shot per dispatch — buf_write_fact_multi dies on preexist.
#
# Args: <oauth_access_token>
zrbgp_depot_state_emit() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "zrbgp_depot_state_emit: token required"

  # RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID is min=0 in the regime (optional for payor-only
  # operations) but required here to enumerate depot projects.
  test -n "${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID:-}" \
    || buc_die "RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID is not set — required for depot_list"

  local -r z_billing_url_base="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBILLING}${RBGC_CLOUDBILLING_V1}/billingAccounts/${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID}/projects"

  # Per-iteration synthesized locals (BCG exception 2 — declare outside, assign inside)
  local z_billing_page=1
  local z_billing_page_token=""
  local z_billing_url=""
  local z_billing_tok_enc=""
  local z_billing_infix=""
  local z_project_count=0
  local z_index=0
  local z_global_project_index=0
  local z_project_id=""
  local z_crm_infix=""
  local z_crm_code=""
  local z_lifecycle_state=""
  local z_state=""
  local z_sa_url_base=""
  local z_sa_url=""
  local z_sa_page=1
  local z_sa_page_token=""
  local z_sa_tok_enc=""
  local z_sa_infix=""
  local z_sa_list_code=""
  local z_sa_count=0
  local z_sa_index=0
  local z_sa_email=""
  local z_mason_email=""
  local z_mason_local=""
  local z_moniker=""

  while :; do
    z_billing_url="${z_billing_url_base}"
    if test -n "${z_billing_page_token}"; then
      z_billing_tok_enc=$(rbgu_urlencode_capture "${z_billing_page_token}") \
        || buc_die "Failed to URL-encode Billing v1 pageToken"
      z_billing_url="${z_billing_url}?pageToken=${z_billing_tok_enc}"
    fi

    z_billing_infix="depot_state_billing_${z_billing_page}"
    rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_billing_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_billing_infix}"
    rbgu_http_require_ok "List billing account projects" "${z_billing_infix}"

    z_project_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_billing_infix}" '.projectBillingInfo // [] | length') \
      || z_project_count=0

    z_index=0
    while test "${z_index}" -lt "${z_project_count}"; do
      z_project_id=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_billing_infix}" ".projectBillingInfo[${z_index}].projectId") \
        || { z_index=$((z_index + 1)); z_global_project_index=$((z_global_project_index + 1)); continue; }

      # CRM v1 lifecycle lookup gates the SA list — cheaper call, single
      # classification decision before potentially-failing IAM scan.
      z_crm_infix="depot_state_crm_${z_global_project_index}"
      rbgu_http_json "GET" \
        "${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/projects/${z_project_id}" \
        "${z_token}" "${z_crm_infix}" || true

      z_crm_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_crm_infix}") || z_crm_code=""
      if test "${z_crm_code}" != "200"; then
        buc_log_args "Skipping project (CRM lookup HTTP ${z_crm_code}): ${z_project_id}"
        z_index=$((z_index + 1))
        z_global_project_index=$((z_global_project_index + 1))
        continue
      fi

      z_lifecycle_state=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_crm_infix}" '.lifecycleState') \
        || z_lifecycle_state=""

      if test "${z_lifecycle_state}" = "DELETE_REQUESTED"; then
        z_state="${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED}"
      elif test "${z_lifecycle_state}" = "ACTIVE"; then
        z_state="${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE}"
      else
        buc_log_args "Skipping project ${z_project_id} with unrecognized lifecycle state: ${z_lifecycle_state}"
        z_index=$((z_index + 1))
        z_global_project_index=$((z_global_project_index + 1))
        continue
      fi

      # Scan SA list for a Mason SA — moniker portion anchored to RBRR's
      # ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$ shape.  Paginated via nextPageToken; break out on
      # first hit since the depot's Mason SA is a singleton.
      z_sa_url_base="${RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM}${RBGC_IAM_V1}/projects/${z_project_id}/serviceAccounts"
      z_sa_page=1
      z_sa_page_token=""
      z_mason_email=""

      while :; do
        z_sa_url="${z_sa_url_base}"
        if test -n "${z_sa_page_token}"; then
          z_sa_tok_enc=$(rbgu_urlencode_capture "${z_sa_page_token}") \
            || buc_die "Failed to URL-encode IAM v1 pageToken"
          z_sa_url="${z_sa_url}?pageToken=${z_sa_tok_enc}"
        fi

        z_sa_infix="depot_state_sa_${z_global_project_index}_${z_sa_page}"
        rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_sa_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_sa_infix}" || true

        z_sa_list_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_sa_infix}") || z_sa_list_code=""
        if test "${z_sa_list_code}" != "200"; then
          buc_log_args "Skipping project (SA list HTTP ${z_sa_list_code}): ${z_project_id}"
          break
        fi

        z_sa_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_sa_infix}" '.accounts // [] | length') \
          || z_sa_count=0

        z_sa_index=0
        while test "${z_sa_index}" -lt "${z_sa_count}"; do
          z_sa_email=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_sa_infix}" ".accounts[${z_sa_index}].email") \
            || { z_sa_index=$((z_sa_index + 1)); continue; }
          if [[ "${z_sa_email}" =~ ^${RBCC_role_mason}-[a-z][a-z0-9]*@[^@]*\.iam\.gserviceaccount\.com$ ]]; then
            z_mason_email="${z_sa_email}"
            break
          fi
          z_sa_index=$((z_sa_index + 1))
        done

        test -z "${z_mason_email}" || break

        z_sa_page_token=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_sa_infix}" '.nextPageToken') \
          || z_sa_page_token=""
        test -n "${z_sa_page_token}" || break
        z_sa_page=$((z_sa_page + 1))
      done

      if test -z "${z_mason_email}"; then
        buc_log_args "Skipping project — no Mason SA found: ${z_project_id}"
        z_index=$((z_index + 1))
        z_global_project_index=$((z_global_project_index + 1))
        continue
      fi

      # Derive moniker from Mason SA local-part (strip leading mason- prefix)
      z_mason_local="${z_mason_email%%@*}"
      z_moniker="${z_mason_local#"${RBCC_role_mason}-"}"

      buf_write_fact_multi "${z_moniker}" "${RBCC_fact_ext_depot}"         "${z_state}"
      buf_write_fact_multi "${z_moniker}" "${RBCC_fact_ext_depot_project}" "${z_project_id}"

      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
      z_global_project_index=$((z_global_project_index + 1))
    done

    z_billing_page_token=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_billing_infix}" '.nextPageToken') \
      || z_billing_page_token=""
    test -n "${z_billing_page_token}" || break
    z_billing_page=$((z_billing_page + 1))
  done
}

# Post-lifecycle hook: refresh per-moniker depot fact files.
# Called by levy and unmake at end-of-operation so observers (theurge
# autodetect, manual inspection) see fresh state.
zrbgp_depot_list_update() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  buc_log_args "Refreshing per-moniker depot fact files"

  local z_token
  z_token=$(zrbgp_authenticate_capture) || buc_die "Failed to authenticate for depot fact refresh"

  zrbgp_depot_state_emit "${z_token}"
}

######################################################################
# External Functions (rbgp_*)

zrbgp_billing_attach() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  local -r z_billing_account="${1:-}"

  buc_doc_brief "Attach a billing account to the project"
  buc_doc_param "billing_account" "Billing account ID to attach"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${z_billing_account}" || buc_die "Billing account ID required"

  buc_step "Attaching billing account: ${z_billing_account}"

  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"
  local -r z_billing_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_billing_attach.json"
  jq -n --arg billingAccountName "billingAccounts/${z_billing_account}" \
    --arg projectId "${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}" \
    '{
      billingAccountName: $billingAccountName,
      projectId: $projectId,
      billingEnabled: true
    }' > "${z_billing_body}" || buc_die "Failed to build billing attach body"

  local -r z_billing_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}:setBillingInfo"
  rbgu_http_json "PUT" "${z_billing_url}" "${z_token}" \
                                  "${ZRBGP_INFIX_BILLING_ATTACH}" "${z_billing_body}"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "Attach billing account" "${ZRBGP_INFIX_BILLING_ATTACH}"

  buc_success "Billing account ${z_billing_account} attached to project"
}

zrbgp_billing_detach() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Detach billing account from the project"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step "Detaching billing account from project"

  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"
  local -r z_billing_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_billing_detach.json"
  jq -n --arg projectId "${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}" \
    '{
      projectId: $projectId,
      billingEnabled: false
    }' > "${z_billing_body}" || buc_die "Failed to build billing detach body"

  local -r z_billing_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}:setBillingInfo"
  rbgu_http_json "PUT" "${z_billing_url}" "${z_token}" \
                                  "${ZRBGP_INFIX_BILLING_DETACH}" "${z_billing_body}"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "Detach billing account" "${ZRBGP_INFIX_BILLING_DETACH}"

  buc_success "Billing account detached from project"
}



zrbgp_liens_list() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "List all liens on the project"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step "Listing liens on project: ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"

  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"
  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/liens?parent=projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGP_INFIX_LIST_LIENS}"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "List liens" "${ZRBGP_INFIX_LIST_LIENS}"

  local z_lien_count
  z_lien_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${ZRBGP_INFIX_LIST_LIENS}" '.liens // [] | length') || buc_die "Failed to parse liens response"

  if test "${z_lien_count}" -eq 0; then
    buc_info "No liens found on project"
    return 0
  fi

  buc_step "Found ${z_lien_count} lien(s):"
  jq -r '.liens[]? | "  - " + .name + " (reason: " + .reason + ")"' \
    "${ZRBGU_PREFIX}${ZRBGP_INFIX_LIST_LIENS}${ZRBGU_POSTFIX_JSON}" || true

  return 0
}

zrbgp_lien_delete() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  local -r z_lien_name="${1:-}"

  buc_doc_brief "Delete a specific lien from the project"
  buc_doc_param "lien_name" "Full resource name of the lien to delete"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${z_lien_name}" || buc_die "Lien name required"

  buc_step "Deleting lien: ${z_lien_name}"

  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"
  rbgu_http_json "DELETE" "${RBGC_API_CRM_DELETE_LIEN}/${z_lien_name}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGP_INFIX_DELETE_LIEN}"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "Delete lien" "${ZRBGP_INFIX_DELETE_LIEN}" 404 "not found (already deleted)"

  buc_success "Lien deleted: ${z_lien_name}"
}


######################################################################
# Capture: list required services that are NOT enabled (blank = all enabled)
zrbgp_required_apis_missing_capture() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_token}" || { echo ""; return 1; }

  local z_missing=""
  local z_api=""
  local z_service=""
  local z_infix=""
  local z_state=""
  local z_code=""

  for z_api in                       \
    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_CRM}"      \
    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_GAR}"      \
    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_IAM}"      \
